front: Building: exterior clad in undulating titanium sheets resembling fish scales; sits on the Nervion River in Bilbao, in Basque Country
Building: central atrium nicknamed 'the flower'; described as a 'metaphoric city'
Building: Jeff Koons's Puppy topiary dog and Louise Bourgeois's Maman spider stand outside; Richard Serra's Matter of Time is displayed inside
Building: home of the Los Angeles Philharmonic; concave wave-like stainless-steel exterior
Building: stainless-steel panels had to be dulled in 2005 because they reflected 140-degree heat onto neighboring buildings
Building: interior designed as a 'living room for the city,' contrasting with the cozier Dorothy Chandler Pavilion
Building: Esa-Pekka Salonen composed Wing on Wing for its opening — scored for two sopranos and orchestra, includes recordings of a fish and the architect's voice
Building: John Adams's The Dharma at Big Sur premiered at its opening, conducted by Salonen; two movements are A New Day and Sri Moonstone
Building: two towers in Prague evoking Fred Astaire and Ginger Rogers' dance movements; designed with Czech architect Vlado Milunic
Building: a twisted metal sculpture on top is nicknamed 'Medusa'; Jean Nouvel initially turned down the commission

back: Museum of Pop Culture / Experience Music Project (Frank Gehry)
work: Museum of Pop Culture / Experience Music Project (Seattle)

front: Building: red, black, and white sections inspired by a smashed guitar; a monorail passes through it; built for Microsoft co-founder Paul Allen in Seattle
back: Museum of Pop Culture / Experience Music Project (Frank Gehry)
work: Museum of Pop Culture / Experience Music Project (Seattle)

front: Building: a spiral-shaped sculpture made of hundreds of guitars is a focal point inside
back: Museum of Pop Culture / Experience Music Project (Frank Gehry)

front: Architect: Canadian-American Deconstructivist; won the Pritzker Architecture Prize in 1989
Architect: recurring fish motif — El Peix golden fish sculpture (Barcelona 1992 Olympics), Glass Standing Fish at Weisman Art Museum, fish dance restaurant in Kobe
Architect: Binoculars Building (Chiat/Day, Venice CA) — giant binoculars at entrance; designed with Claes Oldenburg and Coosje van Bruggen
Architect: Jay Pritzker Pavilion in Chicago's Millennium Park — criss-crossing pipes covered in speakers as a trellis; classified as art to prevent removal from Grant Park
Architect: Stata Center at MIT — sued for moisture and water leakage
Architect: corrugated cardboard 'Easy Edges' furniture; 1992 bentwood furniture series with each piece named after a hockey term
Architect: Santa Monica residence extends a Dutch Colonial structure using corrugated steel and mesh fencing in an 'open-ended' approach
Architect: Dwight D. Eisenhower Memorial — originally conceived as a 'roofless' temple; mesh tapestry depicting the French coast framed by eight large stone columns
Architect: Lou Ruvo Center for Brain Health (Las Vegas); initially refused Las Vegas commissions until accepting this one
Architect: Vitra Design Museum on the Vitra campus in Weil am Rhein, Germany
Architect: 8 Spruce Street (Manhattan) — tallest residential tower in Western hemisphere at completion; first five floors tan brick, used as a school
Architect: UTS Chau Chak Wing Building in Sydney — mocked as resembling a brown paper bag
Architect: Biomuseo natural history museum on Panama City's Causeway Islands, designed for the Smithsonian Tropical Research Institute
